one_line: Bengali revolutionary leader who died fighting British forces at Balasore in 1915.

appearance.age_look: Early 30s, tall and broad-shouldered with a powerful wrestler’s build.
appearance.face: Oval face with strong jaw, medium-brown complexion, straight nose, thick dark brows, a neatly trimmed moustache, and intense yet calm eyes; default expression is composed and determined, often with a slight, resolute frown.
appearance.hair: Thick, straight, dark-black hair combed back from the forehead in a short side-part, cut just above the ears and neatly maintained, no headwear.
appearance.costume: Simple early-20th-century Indian nationalist attire: an off-white handspun khadi kurta (knee-length, straight cut, long sleeves, round neck with short placket) over a slightly darker natural-beige dhoti wrapped cleanly to mid-calf, the dhoti pleats tied at the front in Bengali style; bare feet or simple brown leather sandals; a light, undyed khadi gamchha (thin towel) with faint red border casually looped once around his neck or hanging over one shoulder; no jewellery except a slim black-thread wrist cord on his right hand; fabric feels coarse but clean, with only very subtle vertical weave texture and no printed patterns.
appearance.signature: A well-used British-service-pattern bolt-action rifle (Lee-Enfield style) with a dark wooden stock and metal barrel, slung diagonally across his back with a plain brown leather strap, occasionally held ready in his hands.

ref_desc: Bagha Jatin is a tall, broad-shouldered man in his early 30s with medium-brown skin, short black hair combed back, a neat moustache, wearing an off-white khadi kurta, beige dhoti, simple sandals, a light gamchha, and carrying a dark-wood bolt-action rifle on a brown strap.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Bagha Jatin as a dignified, realistic 1915 Bengali revolutionary: a tall, broad-shouldered man in his early 30s with medium-brown complexion, an oval face, strong jaw, intense calm eyes, straight nose, thick dark brows, and a neatly trimmed moustache, his thick straight black hair cut short above the ears and combed back from a subtle side-part with no headwear; he wears a simple off-white handspun khadi kurta, knee-length with long sleeves and a round neck with a short placket, over a slightly darker natural-beige dhoti wrapped Bengali style to mid-calf, the pleats tied at the front, with either bare feet or plain brown leather sandals, and a light undyed khadi gamchha with a faint muted-red border looped once around his neck or hanging from one shoulder, plus a slim black-thread cord on his right wrist; his single signature weapon is a realistic early-20th-century British bolt-action rifle with a dark wooden stock and metal barrel, slung diagonally across his back on a plain brown leather strap or held in his hands, and the overall colour palette stays to off-white, natural beige, dark brown, black, and muted red, shown from multiple angles to emphasize consistent face, hair, clothing details, and the rifle.
